steps.title: Identify the triangle configuration
steps.content: We have triangle XYZ where angle X measures 52°, side XY = 24 units, and side XZ = 17 units. These are two sides emanating from vertex X with the angle between them known — this is a Side-Angle-Side (SAS) configuration.

steps.title: Apply the SAS area formula
steps.content:
When two sides and the included angle are known, the area is:
$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2}ab\sin C$$
where $a$ and $b$ are the two sides and $C$ is the included angle. Substituting our values:
$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 24 \cdot 17 \cdot \sin 52°$$

steps.title: Simplify to find the area
steps.content:
Multiply the constants:
$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 408 \cdot \sin 52°$$
$$\text{Area} = 204 \sin 52°$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
